title: Deinostigma minutihamata
what_description: Deinostigma minutihamata is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, native to China. It is a herbaceous perennial with small, ovate leaves that are 2.5-4.5 cm long and 1.5-2.5 cm wide, having a short petiole and a crenate to dentate margin. The leaves are densely hairy on both surfaces, with minute hairs being 0.1-0.2 mm in length. Deinostigma minutihamata produces small, white to pale yellow flowers in a raceme, each 2-4 cm in length, with a tubular corolla and four stamens. The flowers are surrounded by two bracts that are 1-1.5 cm long and 0.5-1 cm wide, which are densely hairy and ciliate.

This plant also possesses antioxidant properties, which are due to the presence of phenolic compounds, allowing it to scavenge free radicals and protect cells from oxidative damage. Furthermore, Deinostigma minutihamata has been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which are mediated by saponins and glycosides, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate associated symptoms. constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Deinostigma minutihamata are quercetin, flavonoids, kaempferol, phenolic acids, and alkaloids. Quercetin, a key flavonoid, exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, while flavonoids demonstrate cardiovascular benefits and cancer prevention. preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Deinostigma minutihamata are tea, infusion, tincture, decoction, and salve. Tea is a preparation of the leaves, which are steeped in hot water to extract their medicinal properties. Infusion involves steeping the leaves in cold water, often used for its calming effects. Tincture is a concentrated liquid extract of the leaves made using a solvent, commonly used for its antiseptic properties. Decoction is a preparation of the roots, which are boiled in water to release their medicinal compounds, often used to treat digestive issues, while salve is a topical preparation of the plant's extracts, used to treat skin conditions and wounds.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Deinostigma minutihamata improperly include experiencing nausea and inducing nausea, which can be attributed to the plant's toxic compounds, particularly glycosides and alkaloids. These substances can cause gastrointestinal distress, leading to developing abdominal pain and causing diarrhea, a condition exacerbated by the plant's irritant properties on the digestive system. The improper use of Deinostigma minutihamata can also result in severe gastrointestinal issues due to its potential toxicity and irritant effects. In some cases, the plant's toxic compounds can cause vomiting and severe abdominal pain, which can be life-threatening if left untreated. It is essential to handle and use Deinostigma minutihamata with caution and under the guidance of a medical professional to minimize the risk of adverse effects.

taxonomy: Deinostigma minutihamata is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. The classification of Deinostigma minutihamata is as follows: it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperms, the clade Eudicots, the clade Asterids, the order Lamiales, the family Gesneriaceae, the genus Deinostigma, and the species D. minutihamata. intro: Deinostigma minutihamata, commonly known as the 'mini-hamata orchid', is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may help to alleviate symptoms of certain diseases such as arthritis and reduce oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Deinostigma minutihamata include phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Deinostigma minutihamata is used in herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever and digestive issues.
